behavior_summary: This script implements a minimal 'U8P_TROUGHNS::look' (examine) handler for the object/NPC TROUGHNS. When the player inspects or 'looks at' this entity, the script emits a single short bark text 'trough ' and then suspends/ends the U8P_TROUGHNS::look process.

There is no branching, no menus, no reads or writes to global flags, and no external engine calls detected. The behavior is purely descriptive and intended to provide the short label shown to the player on inspection.
The presence of a suspend/terminate node is inferred from the decompilation; thus after producing the bark the process yields or finishes.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TROUGHNS::look: start -> Bark('trough ') -> suspend/terminate (process yields or ends).
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text 'trough ' intentional (formatting for concatenation) or an artifact of decompilation?

Is this handler also invoked via a fast-area entry (enterFastArea) in addition to an explicit 'U8P_TROUGHNS::look' action, as other U8P_TROUGHNS::look handlers sometimes are? The decompilation hints at fast-area semantics but does not show explicit fast-area nodes.

main_function.name: U8P_TROUGHNS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive label when the player examines/looks at the TROUGHNS object/NPC by emitting a single bark 'trough ' and then suspending or ending the U8P_TROUGHNS::look process.
entry_conditions: Player performs a 'U8P_TROUGHNS::look' (examine) action on this object/NPC.
Inferred: may also be invoked by entering the object's 'fast-area' U8P_TROUGHNS::look trigger region (enterFastArea).

function_map.name: U8P_TROUGHNS::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_TROUGHNS::look' (examine) interaction; emit short descriptive bark and end the process.
